title: Bromus incanus
intro: Bromus incanus, commonly known as brown bent or rough brome, is a species of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which are attributed to its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and support immune system function. The bioactive constituents of Bromus incanus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health issues, including colds, coughs, and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Bromus incanus is a species of the genus Bromus. The genus Bromus belongs to the family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family. The family Poaceae is a part of the monocot order Poales. This classification indicates that Bromus incanus is a type of monocot plant. The Poales order is further classified under the larger class Liliopsida.
